methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $113M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Pickle Robot Company is a Series B robotics and manufacturing firm that has raised $113M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of nine private firms and one public company in the robotics sector, with particular weight on six same-stage Series B peers including 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy for post-money value, applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implying a valuation of $215M. Peer-median methodology references the six same-sector, same-stage comparables, which span a range of $300M to $10.0B with a median valuation of $1.1B, yielding an implied value of $1.1B. The wide peer range ($300M–$10.0B) reflects material heterogeneity in company scale and investor appetite within the robotics space.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $182M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $645M. The substantial gap between stage-progression and peer-median methods reflects uncertainty about where Pickle Robot fits within the competitive maturity spectrum. Key sensitivities include peer selection (the $10.0B outlier materially shifts medians) and evidence of commercial traction relative to reference companies. Additional revenue and unit economics data would narrow this range considerably.
